mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations expand.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, translating into stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops by 0.5pp over 3 months during recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales combined with strong payroll employment indicates healthy consumer demand and a strong labor market, which together drive corporate revenue and profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative when both RSAFS and PAYEMS growth conditions are met, over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production, especially when the economy is already weak, signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO declines for two consecutive months during a late-cycle or recessionary period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts indicates strong demand for new homes and construction activity, which has broad positive spillover effects across the economy, boosting consumer wealth and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
regime_hint: any regime with positive job growth
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is not statistically significant from zero (p > 0.1) when PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and can lead to tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting corporate earnings, thus leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates a robust and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, thereby supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized.
